_meta.title: Known Data Breach Incidents
_meta.note: All incidents are real and publicly documented. Details sourced from public breach reports, SEC filings, and regulatory disclosures.

incidents.name: Equifax Data Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Names, SSNs, dates of birth, addresses, driver's license numbers, credit card numbers (209,000)
incidents.rootCause: Unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability (CVE-2017-5638) exploited to access databases. Data stored unencrypted in some systems.
incidents.vector: Web application vulnerability (SQL injection via Struts)
failedControls: Encryption at rest for sensitive data
incidents.financialImpact: $1.4 billion total cost, $700 million FTC settlement
lessonsLearned: Encrypt all PII at rest, not just PCI-scoped data
Implement database activity monitoring
Patch critical vulnerabilities within 48 hours
Segment databases containing sensitive data

incidents.name: Capital One Data Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Names, addresses, credit scores, credit limits, balances, SSNs (140,000), bank account numbers (80,000)
incidents.rootCause: Misconfigured WAF on AWS allowed SSRF attack to access IAM role credentials, then used to access S3 buckets containing customer data.
incidents.vector: Cloud misconfiguration (SSRF + overly permissive IAM role)
lessonsLearned: Implement least-privilege IAM for cloud services
Deploy cloud DLP to detect mass data access
Encrypt sensitive data with customer-managed keys
Monitor for anomalous cloud API calls

incidents.name: Marriott International Data Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Names, addresses, phone numbers, email addresses, passport numbers (5.25 million), encrypted payment card numbers, travel history
incidents.rootCause: Starwood reservation system compromised since 2014, undetected through Marriott acquisition. Attackers had persistent access to database.
incidents.vector: Advanced persistent threat with database access
incidents.financialImpact: $124 million GDPR fine (reduced from $28M), class action settlements
lessonsLearned: Conduct thorough security assessment during M&A
Implement database activity monitoring for anomaly detection
Encrypt all PII fields at column level
Enforce data retention policies to minimize exposure

incidents.name: Yahoo Data Breaches
incidents.dataExposed: Names, email addresses, dates of birth, telephone numbers, hashed passwords (MD5), security questions/answers
incidents.rootCause: State-sponsored attackers accessed user database. Weak password hashing (MD5 without salt for many accounts). Second breach in 2014 affected 500 million accounts.
incidents.financialImpact: $350 million reduction in Verizon acquisition price, $117.5 million settlement
lessonsLearned: Use modern password hashing (bcrypt, Argon2)
Encrypt sensitive fields beyond just passwords
Implement real-time database access monitoring
Establish breach detection and response capabilities

incidents.name: T-Mobile Data Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Names, billing addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, dates of birth, T-Mobile account numbers
incidents.rootCause: Attacker exploited a single API to access customer data over approximately six weeks before detection. API returned excessive data.
incidents.financialImpact: Ongoing regulatory scrutiny, $350 million settlement from 2021 breach, additional costs
lessonsLearned: Implement API rate limiting and anomaly detection
Apply data minimization to API responses
Deploy API-specific DLP monitoring
Conduct regular API security testing

incidents.name: MOVEit Transfer Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Varied by organization — employee data, financial records, healthcare data, government records
incidents.rootCause: Zero-day SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2023-34362) in MOVEit Transfer file transfer software exploited by Cl0p ransomware group.
incidents.vector: Supply chain attack via zero-day SQL injection in file transfer software
failedControls: Network segmentation for file transfer systems
incidents.financialImpact: Estimated $10+ billion across all affected organizations
lessonsLearned: Assess security of file transfer infrastructure
Encrypt files before transfer (client-side encryption)
Monitor file transfer systems for anomalous activity
Implement zero-trust for file transfer networks

incidents.name: Optus Data Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Names, dates of birth, phone numbers, email addresses, physical addresses, ID document numbers (passport, driver's license, Medicare)
incidents.rootCause: Unauthenticated API endpoint exposed customer data. No authentication required to access the API.
incidents.financialImpact: A$140 million class action, regulatory investigation by OAIC
lessonsLearned: Enforce authentication on all APIs
Conduct regular API security assessments
Implement API gateway with authentication enforcement
Minimize data returned by APIs

incidents.name: Medibank Data Breach
incidents.dataExposed: Names, dates of birth, Medicare numbers, health claims data including diagnosis codes and procedures
incidents.rootCause: Stolen contractor credentials used to access internal systems and extract customer and health claims data. REvil-linked attackers published data on dark web after ransom refusal.
failedControls: DLP for detecting bulk data extraction
incidents.financialImpact: A$46.4 million direct costs, significant reputational damage
lessonsLearned: Enforce MFA on all remote access including contractors
Implement PAM for third-party access
Deploy DAM to detect mass data extraction
Classify and encrypt health data at column level
